# claude-skill-jsonld-knowledge-graph

> A Claude Code skill (documentation-only, MIT, v0.1.0) that designs and ships a companion JSON-LD knowledge graph (`graph.jsonld`) next to `llms.txt` for projects with stable concept-level structure. Encodes domain entities and relationships as schema.org triples so LLM search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude) can cite the structure machine-readably. Pairs with `llms-txt-writer` (which owns prose / navigator wording).

## Core documentation

- [SKILL.md](skills/jsonld-knowledge-graph/SKILL.md): full skill specification — when-to-use gate, 9 reusable design moves, schema vocabulary design criteria, cross-graph `@id` discipline, bilingual strategy, companion file wiring, verification workflow, Hugging Face Datasets mirror sync, maintenance contract
- [inspiration.md](skills/jsonld-knowledge-graph/inspiration.md): origin story and canonical implementation pointers (kept separate from `SKILL.md` for portability)
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (install, how it works, key concept, related skills)
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release notes (v0.1.0 initial release)

## The 9 design moves

The skill prescribes nine reusable moves that turn an ad-hoc JSON-LD file into a stable, LLM-citable artifact:

1. Dual `@type` on every node (custom namespace + schema.org base)
2. Language-tagged literals for bilingual (`@language` per `@value`, single file for en/ja)
3. Schema absence enforces forbidden relationships (don't define edge types you must never use)
4. Cross-graph `@id` reuse so JSON-LD processors merge triples across files
5. Volatile state (version, count, churning enums) excluded at schema level
6. Matrix encoding via paired complementary edges (e.g. `appliesTo` + `realizedBy`)
7. Root `Dataset` node so crawlers see the graph's purpose and entry points
8. Reading-order block in `llms.txt` directing AI to read `graph.jsonld` first
9. Reverse-link from line repos to hub graph in hub-and-spoke topology

## Hugging Face Datasets mirror sync

When `graph.jsonld` is updated and pushed to GitHub, the skill recommends also pushing to a Hugging Face Datasets mirror. HF acts as a primary ingest source for LLM training pipelines and knowledge-graph crawlers, and HF's Auto-converted-to-Parquet makes the graph loadable from `pandas` / `Polars` / `Datasets` directly. The workflow flattens `@graph` array to `.jsonl` (one node per line) via `jq -c`, pushes both `graph.jsonld` and `graph.jsonl` via `hf upload`, and keeps the HF dataset card (`README.md`) manually maintained because it carries HF-specific mirror notices. The GitHub repo ↔ HF dataset mapping is project-specific and lives outside `SKILL.md` (in `CLAUDE.md` or `inspiration.md`) to preserve portability.

## Standard reference

- [llmstxt.org](https://llmstxt.org/): Answer.AI's `llms.txt` proposal (Jeremy Howard, 2024) — the two-file `llms.txt` + `llms-full.txt` layout this skill's companion-file wiring extends
- [schema.org](https://schema.org/): the base vocabulary the graph's nodes anchor to (`Dataset`, `ScholarlyArticle`, `DefinedTerm`, `SoftwareSourceCode`, etc.)
- [JSON-LD 1.1](https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld11/): the `@id` reuse and triple-merge behavior the skill depends on

## Sibling skills

- [claude-skill-llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-llms-txt-writer): owns prose / navigator wording for `llms.txt` / `llms-full.txt`. Reads this skill's reading-order block requirement.
- [claude-skill-context-sync](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-context-sync): audits drift between `graph.jsonld` and CODEMAPS (Maintain phase).
- [claude-skill-search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-search-first): research-before-coding workflow.
- [claude-skill-writing-ecosystem](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-writing-ecosystem): human-facing writing orchestrator (different concern: human readers, not AI crawlers).
